text.NABRE: 5:7 Indeed, only with difficulty does one die for a just person, though perhaps for a good person one might even find courage to die.

text.NRSV: But God proves his love for us in that while we still were sinners Christ died for us.
text.Rotherham: But God commendeth his own love unto us in that—we as yet being sinners, Christ in our behalf died.
text.DRB: But God commendeth his charity towards us; because when as yet we were sinners, according to the time,
text.KJV: 5:8 But God commendeth his loue towards vs, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for vs.
text.NABRE: 5:8 But God proves his love for us in that while we were still sinners Christ died for us.

text.NRSV: Much more surely then, now that we have been justified by his blood, will we be saved through him from the wrath of God.
text.Rotherham: Much more, then, having now been declared righteous by his blood, shall we be saved through him from the anger.
text.DRB: Christ died for us; much more therefore, being now justified by his blood, shall we be saved from wrath through him.
text.KJV: 5:9 Much more then being now iustified by his blood, we shalbe saued from wrath through him.
text.NABRE: 5:9 How much more then, since we are now justified by his blood, will we be saved through him from the wrath.

text.NRSV: For if while we were enemies, we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, much more surely, having been reconciled, will we be saved by his life.
text.Rotherham: For, if being enemies we were reconciled unto God through the death of his Son, much more, having been reconciled, shall we be saved by his life.
text.DRB: For if, when we were enemies, we were reconciled to God by the death of his Son; much more, being reconciled, shall we be saved by his life.
text.KJV: 5:10 For if when wee were enemies, we were reconciled to God, by the death of his sonne: much more being reconciled, we shalbe saued by his life.
text.NABRE: 5:10 Indeed, if, while we were enemies, we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, how much more, once reconciled, will we be saved by his life.

text.NRSV: But more than that, we even boast in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now received reconciliation.
text.Rotherham: And, not only, so, but are even boasting in God, through our Lord Jesus [Christ],—through whom, now, the reconciliation we have received.
text.DRB: And not only so; but also we glory in God, through our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom we have now received reconciliation.
text.KJV: 5:11 And not onely so, but wee also ioy in God, through our Lorde Iesus Christ, by whom we haue now receiued the atonement.
text.NABRE: 5:11 Not only that, but we also boast of God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now received reconciliation.

text.NRSV: Therefore, just as sin came into the world through one man, and death came through sin, and so death spread to all because all have sinned—
text.Rotherham: For this cause,—just as, through one man, sin into the world entered, and through sin, death,—and, so, unto all men death passed through, for that all had sinned;—
text.DRB: Wherefore as by one man sin entered into this world, and by sin death; and so death passed upon all men, in whom all have sinned.
text.KJV: 5:12 Wherefore, as by one man sinne entred into the world, and death by sin: and so death passed vpon all men, for that all haue sinned.
text.NABRE: 5:12 Humanity’s Sin Through Adam. Therefore, just as through one person sin entered the world, and through sin, death, and thus death came to all, inasmuch as all sinned —

text.NRSV: sin was indeed in the world before the law, but sin is not reckoned when there is no law.
text.Rotherham: For, until law, sin was in the world, although sin is not reckoned when there is no law,—
text.DRB: For until the law sin was in the world; but sin was not imputed , when the law was not.
text.KJV: 5:13 For vntill the Law sinne was in the world: but sin is not imputed when there is no Law.
text.NABRE: 5:13 for up to the time of the law, sin was in the world, though sin is not accounted when there is no law.

text.NRSV: Yet death exercised dominion from Adam to Moses, even over those whose sins were not like the transgression of Adam, who is a type of the one who was to come.
text.Rotherham: Yet still, death reigned from Adam until Moses, even over them who had not sinned after the likeness of the transgression of Adam,—who is a type of the Coming One;—
text.DRB: But death reigned from Adam unto Moses, even over them also who have not sinned after the similitude of the transgression of Adam, who is a figure of him who was to come.
text.KJV: 5:14 Neuertheles, death reigned from Adam to Moses, euen ouer them that had not sinned after the similitude of Adams transgression, who is the figure of him that was to come:
text.NABRE: 5:14 But death reigned from Adam to Moses, even over those who did not sin after the pattern of the trespass of Adam, who is the type of the one who was to come.

text.NRSV: But the free gift is not like the trespass. For if the many died through the one man’s trespass, much more surely have the grace of God and the free gift in the grace of the one man, Jesus Christ, abounded for the many.
text.Rotherham: But, not as the fault, so, [also] the decree of favour, for, if, by the fault of the one, the many died, much more, the favour of God and the free-gift in favour, by the one man Jesus Christ, unto the many superabounded;
text.DRB: But not as the offence, so also the gift. For if by the offence of one, many died; much more the grace of God, and the gift, by the grace of one man, Jesus Christ, hath abounded unto many.
text.KJV: 5:15 But not as the offence, so also is the free gift: for if through the offence of one, many bee dead: much more the grace of God, and the gift by grace, which is by one man Iesus Christ, hath abounded vnto many.
text.NABRE: 5:15 Grace and Life Through Christ. But the gift is not like the transgression. For if by that one person’s transgression the many died, how much more did the grace of God and the gracious gift of the one person Jesus Christ overflow for the many.

text.NRSV: And the free gift is not like the effect of the one man’s sin. For the judgment following one trespass brought condemnation, but the free gift following many trespasses brings justification.
text.Rotherham: And, not as through one that sinned, is that which is freely given, for, the sentence of judgment, indeed, was—out of one [fault] into condemnation, whereas, the decree of favour, is—out of many faults, into a recovery of righteousness.
text.DRB: And not as it was by one sin, so also is the gift. For judgment indeed was by one unto condemnation; but grace is of many offences, unto justification.
text.KJV: 5:16 And not as it was by one that sinned, so is the gift: for the iudgement was by one to condemnation: but the free gift is of many offences vnto iustification.
text.NABRE: 5:16 And the gift is not like the result of the one person’s sinning. For after one sin there was the judgment that brought condemnation; but the gift, after many transgressions, brought acquittal.

text.NRSV: If, because of the one man’s trespass, death exercised dominion through that one, much more surely will those who receive the abundance of grace and the free gift of righteousness exercise dominion in life through the one man, Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, if, by the fault of the one, death reigned through the one, much more, they who the superabundance of the favour and of [the free-gift of] the righteousness do receive, in life, shall reign through the one, Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: For if by one man's offence death reigned through one; much more they who receive abundance of grace, and of the gift, and of justice, shall reign in life through one, Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 5:17 For if by one mans offence, death raigned by one, much more they which receiue abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousnes, shall reigne in life by one, Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 5:17 For if, by the transgression of one person, death came to reign through that one, how much more will those who receive the abundance of grace and of the gift of justification come to reign in life through the one person Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore just as one man’s trespass led to condemnation for all, so one man’s act of righteousness leads to justification and life for all.
text.Rotherham: Hence then, as through one fault, [the sentence was] unto all men unto condemnation, so, also, through one recovery of righteousness, [the decree of favour] is unto all men for righteous acquittal unto life;
text.DRB: Therefore, as by the offence of one, unto all men to condemnation; so also by the justice of one, unto all men to justification of life.
text.KJV: 5:18 Therfore as by the offence of one, iudgment came vpon all men to condemnation: euen so by the righteousnes of one, the free gift came vpon all men vnto iustification of life.
text.NABRE: 5:18 In conclusion, just as through one transgression condemnation came upon all, so through one righteous act acquittal and life came to all.

text.NRSV: For just as by the one man’s disobedience the many were made sinners, so by the one man’s obedience the many will be made righteous.
text.Rotherham: For, just as, through the disobedience of the one man, sinners, the many were constituted, so, also, through the obedience of the one, righteous, the many shall be constituted—
text.DRB: For as by the disobedience of one man, many were made sinners; so also by the obedience of one, many shall be made just.
text.KJV: 5:19 For as by one mans disobedience many were made sinners: so by the obedience of one, shall many bee made righteous.
text.NABRE: 5:19 For just as through the disobedience of one person the many were made sinners, so through the obedience of one the many will be made righteous.

text.NRSV: But law came in, with the result that the trespass multiplied; but where sin increased, grace abounded all the more,
text.Rotherham: Law, however, gained admission, in order that the fault might abound, but, where the sin abounded, the favour greatly superabounded:
text.DRB: Now the law entered in, that sin might abound . And where sin abounded, grace did more abound.
text.KJV: 5:20 Moreouer, the Lawe entred, that the offence might abound: but where sinne abounded, grace did much more abound.
text.NABRE: 5:20 The law entered in so that transgression might increase but, where sin increased, grace overflowed all the more,

text.NRSV: so that, just as sin exercised dominion in death, so grace might also exercise dominion through justification leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.Rotherham: In order that—just as sin reigned in death, so, also, favour, might reign through righteousness unto life age-abiding, through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.DRB: That as sin hath reigned to death; so also grace might reign by justice unto life everlasting, through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.KJV: 5:21 That as sinne hath reigned vnto death; euen so might grace reigne thorow righteousnes vnto eternall life, by Iesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: 5:21 so that, as sin reigned in death, grace also might reign through justification for eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

text.NRSV: What then are we to say? Should we continue in sin in order that grace may abound?
text.Rotherham: What then shall we say? are we still to continue in sin that favour may abound?
text.DRB: What shall we say, then? shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound?
text.KJV: 6:1 What shall we say then? shall wee continue in sinne: that grace may abound?
text.NABRE: 6:1 IV. Justification and the Christian Life Chapter 6 Freedom from Sin; Life in God. What then shall we say? Shall we persist in sin that grace may abound? Of course not!

text.NRSV: By no means! How can we who died to sin go on living in it?
text.Rotherham: Far be it! we who have died unto sin, how, any longer, shall we live therein?
text.DRB: God forbid. For we that are dead to sin, how shall we live any longer therein?
text.KJV: 6:2 God forbid: how shall wee that are dead to sinne, liue any longer therein?
text.NABRE: 6:2 How can we who died to sin yet live in it?

text.NRSV: Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
text.Rotherham: Or know ye not that, we, as many as were immersed into Christ [Jesus], into his death were immersed?
text.DRB: Know you not that all we, who are baptized in Christ Jesus, are baptized in his death?
text.KJV: 6:3 Know ye not, that so many of vs as were baptized into Iesus Christ, were baptized into his death?
text.NABRE: 6:3 Or are you unaware that we who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?

text.NRSV: Therefore we have been buried with him by baptism into death, so that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, so we too might walk in newness of life.
text.Rotherham: We were, therefore, buried together with him through our immersion into his death, in order that—just as Christ was raised from among the dead through the glory of the Father, so, we also, in newness of life should walk.
text.DRB: For we are buried together with him by baptism into death; that as Christ is risen from the dead by the glory of the Father, so we also may walk in newness of life.
text.KJV: 6:4 Therefore wee are buryed with him by baptisme into death, that like as Christ was raised vp from the dead by the glorie of the Father: euen so wee also should walke in newnesse of life.
text.NABRE: 6:4 We were indeed buried with him through baptism into death, so that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might live in newness of life.

text.NRSV: For if we have been united with him in a death like his, we will certainly be united with him in a resurrection like his.
text.Rotherham: For, if we have come to be grown together in the likeness of his death, certainly, in that of his resurrection also, shall we be.
text.DRB: For if we have been planted together in the likeness of his death, we shall be also in the likeness of his resurrection.
text.KJV: 6:5 For if we haue bene planted together in the likenesse of his death: wee shalbe also in the likenesse of his resurrection:
text.NABRE: 6:5 For if we have grown into union with him through a death like his, we shall also be united with him in the resurrection.

text.NRSV: We know that our old self was crucified with him so that the body of sin might be destroyed, and we might no longer be enslaved to sin.
text.Rotherham: Of this taking note—that, our old man, was crucified together with him in order that the sinful body might be made powerless, that we should, no longer, be in servitude to sin;
text.DRB: Knowing this, that our old man is crucified with him, that the body of sin may be destroyed, to the end that we may serve sin no longer.
text.KJV: 6:6 Knowing this, that our old man is crucified with him, that the bodie of sinne might bee destroyed, that hencefoorth we should not serue sinne.
text.NABRE: 6:6 We know that our old self was crucified with him, so that our sinful body might be done away with, that we might no longer be in slavery to sin.

text.NRSV: For whoever has died is freed from sin.
text.Rotherham: For, he that hath died, hath become righteously acquitted from his sin.
text.DRB: For he that is dead is justified from sin.
text.KJV: 6:7 For he that is dead, is freed from sinne.
text.NABRE: 6:7 For a dead person has been absolved from sin.

text.NRSV: But if we have died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
text.Rotherham: Now, if we have died together with Christ, we believe that we shall also live together with him;
text.DRB: Now if we be dead with Christ, we believe that we shall live also together with Christ:
text.KJV: 6:8 Now if we be dead with Christ, we beleeue that we shal also liue with him:
text.NABRE: 6:8 If, then, we have died with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with him.

text.NRSV: We know that Christ, being raised from the dead, will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him.
text.Rotherham: Knowing that, Christ having been raised from among the dead, no more dieth,—death, over him, no more, hath lordship,—
text.DRB: Knowing that Christ rising again from the dead, dieth now no more, death shall no more have dominion over him.
text.KJV: 6:9 Knowing that Christ being raysed from the dead, dieth no more, death hath no more dominion ouer him.
text.NABRE: 6:9 We know that Christ, raised from the dead, dies no more; death no longer has power over him.

text.NRSV: The death he died, he died to sin, once for all; but the life he lives, he lives to God.
text.Rotherham: For, in that he died, unto sin, died he once for all, but, in that he liveth, he liveth unto God.
text.DRB: For in that he died to sin, he died once; but in that he liveth, he liveth unto God:
text.KJV: 6:10 For in that he dyed, he dyed vnto sinne once: but in that hee liueth, hee liueth vnto God.
text.NABRE: 6:10 As to his death, he died to sin once and for all; as to his life, he lives for God.

text.NRSV: So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: So, ye also, be reckoning yourselves to be—dead indeed unto sin, but, alive unto God in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: So do you also reckon, that you are dead to sin, but alive unto God, in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: 6:11 Likewise reckon yee also your selues to be dead indeed vnto sinne: but aliue vnto God, through Iesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: 6:11 Consequently, you too must think of yourselves as [being] dead to sin and living for God in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Therefore, do not let sin exercise dominion in your mortal bodies, to make you obey their passions.
text.Rotherham: Let not sin, therefore, reign in your death-doomed body, that ye should be obedient to its covetings;
text.DRB: Let no sin therefore reign in your mortal body, so as to obey the lusts thereof.
text.KJV: 6:12 Let not sinne reigne therfore in your mortall body, that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof.
text.NABRE: 6:12 Therefore, sin must not reign over your mortal bodies so that you obey their desires.

text.NRSV: No longer present your members to sin as instruments of wickedness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life, and your members to God as instruments of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Neither be presenting your members as weapons of unrighteousness unto sin, but present yourselves unto God as though alive from among the dead, and your members as weapons of righteousness unto God;
text.DRB: Neither yield ye your members as instruments of iniquity unto sin; but present yourselves to God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of justice unto God.
text.KJV: 6:13 Neither yeeld yee your members as instruments of vnrighteousnes vnto sinne: but yeelde your selues vnto God, as those that are aliue from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousnesse vnto God.
text.NABRE: 6:13 And do not present the parts of your bodies to sin as weapons for wickedness, but present yourselves to God as raised from the dead to life and the parts of your bodies to God as weapons for righteousness.

text.NRSV: For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace.
text.Rotherham: For, sin, over you, shall not have lordship, for ye are not under law, but under favour.
text.DRB: For sin shall not have dominion over you; for you are not under the law, but under grace.
text.KJV: 6:14 For sinne shall not haue dominion ouer you, for yee are not vnder the Law, but vnder Grace.
text.NABRE: 6:14 For sin is not to have any power over you, since you are not under the law but under grace.

text.NRSV: What then? Should we sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!
text.Rotherham: What then? Shall we sin, because we are not under law but under favour? Far be it!
text.DRB: What then? Shall we sin, because we are not under the law, but under grace? God forbid.
text.KJV: 6:15 What then? shal we sinne, because wee are not vnder the Law, but vnder Grace? God forbid.
text.NABRE: 6:15 What then? Shall we sin because we are not under the law but under grace? Of course not!

text.NRSV: Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness?
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that, unto whom ye are presenting yourselves as servants for obedience, servants ye are unto [him unto] whom ye are obedient, whether of sin unto death or of obedience unto righteousness?
text.DRB: Know you not, that to whom you yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants you are whom you obey, whether it be of sin unto death, or of obedience unto justice.
text.KJV: 6:16 Know ye not, that to whom yee yeeld your selues seruants to obey, his seruants ye are to whom ye obey: whether of sinne vnto death, or of obedience vnto righteousnesse?
text.NABRE: 6:16 Do you not know that if you present yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness?

text.NRSV: But thanks be to God that you, having once been slaves of sin, have become obedient from the heart to the form of teaching to which you were entrusted,
text.Rotherham: But thanks be unto God, that—whereas ye were servants of sin, ye became obedient out of the heart unto the mould of teaching into which ye were delivered;
text.DRB: But thanks be to God, that you were the servants of sin, but have obeyed from the heart, unto that form of doctrine, into which you have been delivered.
text.KJV: 6:17 But God bee thanked, that yee were the seruants of sinne: but ye haue obeyed from the heart that fourme of doctrine, which was deliuered you.
text.NABRE: 6:17 But thanks be to God that, although you were once slaves of sin, you have become obedient from the heart to the pattern of teaching to which you were entrusted.

text.NRSV: and that you, having been set free from sin, have become slaves of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: And, being freed from sin, ye were made servants unto righteousness;—
text.DRB: Being then freed from sin, we have been made servants of justice.
text.KJV: 6:18 Being then made free from sinne, yee became the seruants of righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: 6:18 Freed from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.

text.NRSV: I am speaking in human terms because of your natural limitations. For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and to greater and greater iniquity, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness for sanctification.
text.Rotherham: In human fashion am I speaking, because of the weakness of your flesh;—for, just as ye presented your members as servants unto impurity and unto lawlessness [for lawlessness], so now, present ye your members as servants unto righteousness for sanctification.
text.DRB: I speak an human thing, because of the infirmity of your flesh. For as you have yielded your members to serve uncleanness and iniquity, unto iniquity; so now yield your members to serve justice, unto sanctification.
text.KJV: 6:19 I speake after the maner of men, because of the infirmitie of your flesh: for as yee haue yeelded your members seruants to vncleannesse and to iniquitie, vnto iniquitie: euen so now yeelde your members seruants to righteousnesse, vnto holinesse.
text.NABRE: 6:19 I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your nature. For just as you presented the parts of your bodies as slaves to impurity and to lawlessness for lawlessness, so now present them as slaves to righteousness for sanctification.

text.NRSV: When you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
text.Rotherham: For, when ye were servants of sin, ye were free as to righteousness;—
text.DRB: For when you were the servants of sin, you were free men to justice.
text.KJV: 6:20 For when yee were the seruants of sinne ye were free from righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: 6:20 For when you were slaves of sin, you were free from righteousness.

text.NRSV: So what advantage did you then get from the things of which you are now ashamed? The end of those things is death.
text.Rotherham: What fruit, therefore, had ye then—in things for which ye now are taking shame to yourselves? For, the end of those things, is death.
text.DRB: What fruit therefore had you then in those things, of which you are now ashamed? For the end of them is death.
text.KJV: 6:21 What fruit had yee then in those things, whereof ye are now ashamed? for the end of those things is death.
text.NABRE: 6:21 But what profit did you get then from the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.

text.NRSV: But now that you have been set free from sin and have become slaves of God, the advantage you get is sanctification. The end is eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, now, having been freed from sin, and made servants unto God, ye have your fruit for sanctification and, the end, life age-abiding.
text.DRB: But now being made free from sin, and become servants to God, you have your fruit unto sanctification, and the end life everlasting.
text.KJV: 6:22 But now being made free from sinne, and become seruants to God, yee haue your fruit vnto holinesse, and the end euerlasting life.
text.NABRE: 6:22 But now that you have been freed from sin and have become slaves of God, the benefit that you have leads to sanctification, and its end is eternal life.

text.NRSV: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: For, the wages of sin, is death; but, God’s gift of favour, is life age-abiding, in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.DRB: For the wages of sin is death. But the grace of God, life everlasting, in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: 6:23 For the wages of sinne is death: but the gift of God is eternall life, through Iesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: 6:23 For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: Do you not know, brothers and sisters—for I am speaking to those who know the law—that the law is binding on a person only that person is alive?
text.Rotherham: Or are ye ignorant, brethren,—for unto them that understand the law am I speaking, that, the law, hath lordship over a men as long as he liveth?
text.DRB: Know you not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) that the law hath dominion over a man, as long as it liveth ?
text.KJV: 7:1 Know ye not, brethren (for I speake to them that knowe the Lawe) how that the Lawe hath dominion ouer a man, as long as he liueth?
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 Freedom from the Law. Are you unaware, brothers (for I am speaking to people who know the law), that the law has jurisdiction over one as long as one lives?

text.NRSV: Thus a married woman is bound by the law to her husband as long as he lives; but if her husband dies, she is discharged from the law concerning the husband.
text.Rotherham: For, the married woman, unto her living husband is bound by law; but, if her husband have died, she hath received a full release from the law of her husband.
text.DRB: For the woman that hath an husband, whilst her husband liveth is bound to the law. But if her husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.
text.KJV: 7:2 For the woman which hath an husbaud, is bound by the law to her husband, so long as he liueth: but if the husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of the husband.
text.NABRE: 7:2 Thus a married woman is bound by law to her living husband; but if her husband dies, she is released from the law in respect to her husband.

text.NRSV: Accordingly, she will be called an adulteress if she lives with another man while her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free from that law, and if she marries another man, she is not an adulteress.
text.Rotherham: Hence then, her husband being alive, an adulteress, shall she be called—if she become another man’s, but, if the husband have died, she is free from the law; so that she is not an adulteress, though she become another man’s.
text.DRB: Therefore, whilst her husband liveth, she shall be called an adulteress, if she be with another man: but if her husband be dead, she is delivered from the law of her husband; so that she is not an adulteress, if she be with another man.
text.KJV: 7:3 So then if while her husband liueth, shee be married to another man, shee shalbe called an adulteresse: but if her husband be dead, shee is free from that law, so that she is no adulteresse, though she be married to another man.
text.NABRE: 7:3 Consequently, while her husband is alive she will be called an adulteress if she consorts with another man. But if her husband dies she is free from that law, and she is not an adulteress if she consorts with another man.

text.NRSV: In the same way, my friends, you have died to the law through the body of Christ, so that you may belong to another, to him who has been raised from the dead in order that we may bear fruit for God.
text.Rotherham: So, then, my brethren, ye also, were made dead unto the law through the body of the Christ, to the end ye might become another’s—his who from among the dead was raised, in order that we might bring forth fruit unto God.
text.DRB: Therefore, my brethren, you also are become dead to the law, by the body of Christ; that you may belong to another, who is risen again from the dead, that we may bring forth fruit to God.
text.KJV: 7:4 Wherefore my brethren, yee also are become dead to the law by the body of Christ, that ye should be married to another, euen to him who is raised from the dead, that wee should bring forth fruit vnto God,
text.NABRE: 7:4 In the same way, my brothers, you also were put to death to the law through the body of Christ, so that you might belong to another, to the one who was raised from the dead in order that we might bear fruit for God.

text.NRSV: While we were living in the flesh, our sinful passions, aroused by the law, were at work in our members to bear fruit for death.
text.Rotherham: For, when we were in the flesh, the susceptibilities of sins which were through the law, used to be energized in our members unto the bringing forth of fruit unto death;
text.DRB: For when we were in the flesh, the passions of sins, which were by the law, did work in our members, to bring forth fruit unto death.
text.KJV: 7:5 For when wee were in the flesh, the motions of sinnes which were by the law, did worke in our members, to bring foorth fruit vnto death.
text.NABRE: 7:5 For when we were in the flesh, our sinful passions, awakened by the law, worked in our members to bear fruit for death.

text.NRSV: But now we are discharged from the law, dead to that which held us captive, so that we are slaves not under the old written code but in the new life of the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: But, now, we have received full release from the law, by dying [in that] wherein we used to be held fast, so that we should be doing service—in newness of spirit and not in obsoleteness of letter.
text.DRB: But now we are loosed from the law of death, wherein we were detained; so that we should serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.
text.KJV: 7:6 But now wee are deliuered from the law, that being dead wherein we were held, that we should serue in newnesse of spirit, and not in the oldnesse of the letter.
text.NABRE: 7:6 But now we are released from the law, dead to what held us captive, so that we may serve in the newness of the spirit and not under the obsolete letter.

text.NRSV: What then should we say? That the law is sin? By no means! Yet, if it had not been for the law, I would not have known sin. I would not have known what it is to covet if the law had not said, “You shall not covet.”
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall we say? Is the law sin? Far be it! On the contrary, I had not discovered, sin, save through law, for even, of coveting, I had not been aware if, the law, had not kept on saying—Thou shall not covet;
text.DRB: What shall we say, then? Is the law sin? God forbid. But I do not know sin, but by the law; for I had not known concupiscence, if the law did not say: Thou shalt not covet.
text.KJV: 7:7 What shall wee say then? is the law sinne? God forbid. Nay, I had not knowen sinne, but by the lawe: for I had not knowen lust, except the Law had said, Thou shalt not couet.
text.NABRE: 7:7 Acquaintance with Sin Through the Law. What then can we say? That the law is sin? Of course not! Yet I did not know sin except through the law, and I did not know what it is to covet except that the law said, “You shall not covet.”

text.NRSV: But sin, seizing an opportunity in the commandment, produced in me all kinds of covetousness. Apart from the law sin lies dead.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit sin taking, occasion—through the commandment, wrought out in me all manner of coveting; for, apart from law, sin is dead;—
text.DRB: But sin taking occasion by the commandment , wrought in me all manner of concupiscence. For without the law sin was dead.
text.KJV: 7:8 But sinne taking occasion by the commaundement, wrought in me all maner of concupiscence. For without the Law sinne was dead.
text.NABRE: 7:8 But sin, finding an opportunity in the commandment, produced in me every kind of covetousness. Apart from the law sin is dead.

text.NRSV: I was once alive apart from the law, but when the commandment came, sin revived
text.Rotherham: And, I, was alive, apart from law, at one time, but, the commandment coming, sin sprang up to life,
text.DRB: And I lived some time without the law. But when the commandment came, sin revived,
text.KJV: 7:9 For I was aliue without the Law once, but when the commandement came, sinne reuiued, and I died.
text.NABRE: 7:9 I once lived outside the law, but when the commandment came, sin became alive;

text.NRSV: and I died, and the very commandment that promised life proved to be death to me.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, I, died,—and the commandment which was unto life was found by me to be, itself, unto death;
text.DRB: And I died. And the commandment that was ordained to life, the same was found to be unto death to me.
text.KJV: 7:10 And the commandement which was ordained to life, I found to be vnto death.
text.NABRE: 7:10 then I died, and the commandment that was for life turned out to be death for me.

text.NRSV: For sin, seizing an opportunity in the commandment, deceived me and through it killed me.
text.Rotherham: For, sin,—taking occasion—through the commandment, completely deceived me and, through it, slew me:
text.DRB: For sin, taking occasion by the commandment, seduced me, and by it killed me.
text.KJV: 7:11 For sinne taking occasion by the commandement, deceiued me, and by it slew me.
text.NABRE: 7:11 For sin, seizing an opportunity in the commandment, deceived me and through it put me to death.

text.NRSV: So the law is holy, and the commandment is holy and just and good.
text.Rotherham: So that, the law, indeed, is holy, and the commandment, holy, and righteous and good.
text.DRB: Wherefore the law indeed is holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.
text.KJV: 7:12 Wherefore the Law is holy, and the Commandement holy, and iust, and good.
text.NABRE: 7:12 So then the law is holy, and the commandment is holy and righteous and good. Sin and Death.

text.NRSV: Did what is good, then, bring death to me? By no means! It was sin, working death in me through what is good, in order that sin might be shown to be sin, and through the commandment might become sinful beyond measure.
text.Rotherham: Did, then, that which is good, unto me become death? Far be it! But [it was] sin, that it might appear sin, through that which was good unto me working out death, in order that, exceeding sinful, might sin become through the commandment.
text.DRB: Was that then which is good, made death unto me? God forbid. But sin, that it may appear sin , by that which is good, wrought death in me; that sin, by the commandment, might become sinful above measure.
text.KJV: 7:13 Was that then which is good, made death vnto me? God forbid. But sinne, that it might appeare sinne, working death in mee by that which is good: that sinne by the Commaundement might become exceeding sinfull.
text.NABRE: 7:13 Did the good, then, become death for me? Of course not! Sin, in order that it might be shown to be sin, worked death in me through the good, so that sin might become sinful beyond measure through the commandment.

text.NRSV: For we know that the law is spiritual; but I am of the flesh, sold into slavery under sin.
text.Rotherham: For we know that, the law, is spiritual,—I, however, am a creature of flesh, sold under sin;
text.DRB: For we know that the law is spiritual; but I am carnal, sold under sin.
text.KJV: 7:14 For wee know that the Law is spirituall: but I am carnall, sold vnder sinne.
text.NABRE: 7:14 We know that the law is spiritual; but I am carnal, sold into slavery to sin.

text.NRSV: I do not understand my own actions. For I do not do what I want, but I do the very thing I hate.
text.Rotherham: For, that which I am working out, I do not approve,—for not, what I wish, the same I practise, but, what I hate, the same I do:
text.DRB: For that which I work, I understand not. For I do not that good which I will ; but the evil which I hate, that I do.
text.KJV: 7:15 For that which I do, I allow not: for what I would, that do I not, but what I hate, that doe I.
text.NABRE: 7:15 What I do, I do not understand. For I do not do what I want, but I do what I hate.

text.NRSV: Now if I do what I do not want, I agree that the law is good.
text.Rotherham: Now, if what I wish not the same I do, I consent unto the law that [it is] right.
text.DRB: If then I do that which I will not, I consent to the law, that it is good.
text.KJV: 7:16 If then I doe that which I would not, I consent vnto the Law, that it is good.
text.NABRE: 7:16 Now if I do what I do not want, I concur that the law is good.

text.NRSV: But in fact it is no longer I that do it, but sin that dwells within me.
text.Rotherham: Now, however, no longer am, I, working it out, but the, sin, that dwelleth in me:
text.DRB: Now then it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.
text.KJV: 7:17 Now then, it is no more I that doe it: but sinne that dwelleth in me.
text.NABRE: 7:17 So now it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells in me.

text.NRSV: For I know that nothing good dwells within me, that is, in my flesh. I can will what is right, but I cannot do it.
text.Rotherham: I know, in fact, that there dwelleth not in me, that is, in my flesh, anything good; for, the wishing, lieth near me, but, the working out of what is right, not!
text.DRB: For I know that there dwelleth not in me, that is to say, in my flesh, that which is good. For to will, is present with me; but to accomplish that which is good, I find not.
text.KJV: 7:18 For I know, that in me (that is, in my flesh) dwelleth no good thing. For to will is present with me: but how to performe that which is good, I find not.
text.NABRE: 7:18 For I know that good does not dwell in me, that is, in my flesh. The willing is ready at hand, but doing the good is not.

text.NRSV: For I do not do the good I want, but the evil I do not want is what I do.
text.Rotherham: For not, the good that I wish, I do, but, the evil that I do not wish, the same I practise.
text.DRB: For the good which I will, I do not; but the evil which I will not, that I do.
text.KJV: 7:19 For the good that I would, I do not: but the euill which I would not, that I doe.
text.NABRE: 7:19 For I do not do the good I want, but I do the evil I do not want.

text.NRSV: Now if I do what I do not want, it is no longer I that do it, but sin that dwells within me.
text.Rotherham: Now, if what I wish not, the same, I do, no longer am, I, working it out, but the, sin, that dwelleth in me.
text.DRB: Now if I do that which I will not, it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.
text.KJV: 7:20 Now if I doe that I would not, it is no more I that do it, but sinne that dwelleth in me.
text.NABRE: 7:20 Now if [I] do what I do not want, it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells in me.

text.NRSV: So I find it to be a law that when I want to do what is good, evil lies close at hand.
text.Rotherham: Hence, I find the law, to me who wish to be doing the right, that, unto me, the wrong lieth near:
text.DRB: I find then a law, that when I have a will to do good, evil is present with me.
text.KJV: 7:21 I find then a Law, that when I would do good, euil is present with me.
text.NABRE: 7:21 So, then, I discover the principle that when I want to do right, evil is at hand.

text.NRSV: For I delight in the law of God in my inmost self,
text.Rotherham: I have, in fact, a sympathetic pleasure in the law of God; according to the inner man.
text.DRB: For I am delighted with the law of God, according to the inward man:
text.KJV: 7:22 For I delight in the Lawe of God, after the inward man.
text.NABRE: 7:22 For I take delight in the law of God, in my inner self,

text.NRSV: but I see in my members another law at war with the law of my mind, making me captive to the law of sin that dwells in my members.
text.Rotherham: But I behold a diverse law in my members, warring against the law of my mind and taking me captive in the law of sin which existeth in my members:—
text.DRB: But I see another law in my members, fighting against the law of my mind, and captivating me in the law of sin, that is in my members.
text.KJV: 7:23 But I see another Lawe in my members, warring against the Lawe of my minde, and bringing me into captiuity to the Law of sinne, which is in my members.
text.NABRE: 7:23 but I see in my members another principle at war with the law of my mind, taking me captive to the law of sin that dwells in my members.

text.NRSV: Wretched man that I am! Who will rescue me from this body of death?
text.Rotherham: Wretched, man am, I! Who shall rescue me out of this body doomed to death?
text.DRB: Unhappy man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?
text.KJV: 7:24 O wretched man that I am: who shall deliuer me from the body of this death?
text.NABRE: 7:24 Miserable one that I am! Who will deliver me from this mortal body?

text.NRSV: Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then, with my mind I am a slave to the law of God, but with my flesh I am a slave to the law of sin.
text.Rotherham: [But] thanks be unto God!—Through Jesus Christ our Lord. Hence, then,—I myself, with the mind, indeed, am in servitude unto a law of God; but; with the flesh; unto a law of sin.
text.DRB: The grace of God, by Jesus Christ our Lord. Therefore, I myself, with the mind serve the law of God; but with the flesh, the law of sin.
text.KJV: 7:25 I thanke God through Iesus Christ our Lord. So then, with the mind I my self serue the Law of God: but with the flesh, the law of sinne.
text.NABRE: 7:25 Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord. Therefore, I myself, with my mind, serve the law of God but, with my flesh, the law of sin.

text.NRSV: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Hence there is now, no, condemnation unto them who are in Christ Jesus;
text.DRB: There is now therefore no condemnation to them that are in Christ Jesus, who walk not according to the flesh.
text.KJV: 8:1 There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Iesus, who walke not after the flesh, but after the spirit.
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 The Flesh and the Spirit. Hence, now there is no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and of death.
text.Rotherham: For, the law of the spirit of life in Christ Jesus, hath set thee free from the law of sin and of death;—
text.DRB: For the law of the spirit of life, in Christ Jesus, hath delivered me from the law of sin and of death.
text.KJV: 8:2 For the law of the spirit of life, in Christ Iesus, hath made me free from the law of sinne and death.
text.NABRE: 8:2 For the law of the spirit of life in Christ Jesus has freed you from the law of sin and death.

text.NRSV: For God has done what the law, weakened by the flesh, could not do: by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and to deal with sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,
text.Rotherham: For, what was impossible by the law in that it was weak through the flesh, God, by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and concerning sin, condemned sin in the flesh,
text.DRB: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh; God sending his own Son, in the likeness of sinful flesh and of sin, hath condemned sin in the flesh;
text.KJV: 8:3 For what the law could not doe, in that it was weake through the flesh, God sending his owne Sonne, in the likenesse of sinnefull flesh, and for sinne condemned sinne in the flesh:
text.NABRE: 8:3 For what the law, weakened by the flesh, was powerless to do, this God has done: by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for the sake of sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,

text.NRSV: so that the just requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: In order that, the righteous requirement of the law, might be fulfilled in us—who, not according to flesh, do walk, but according to spirit;
text.DRB: That the justification of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh, but according to the spirit.
text.KJV: 8:4 That the righteousnesse of the law might be fulfilled in vs, who walke not after the flesh, but after the spirit.
text.NABRE: 8:4 so that the righteous decree of the law might be fulfilled in us, who live not according to the flesh but according to the spirit.

text.NRSV: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit set their minds on the things of the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For, they who according to flesh have their being, the things of the flesh do prefer, but, they according to the spirit, the things of the spirit;
text.DRB: For they that are according to the flesh, mind the things that are of the flesh; but they that are according to the spirit, mind the things that are of the spirit.
text.KJV: 8:5 For they that are after the flesh, doe minde the things of the flesh: but they that are after the spirit, the things of the spirit.
text.NABRE: 8:5 For those who live according to the flesh are concerned with the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the spirit with the things of the spirit.

text.NRSV: To set the mind on the flesh is death, but to set the mind on the Spirit is life and peace.
text.Rotherham: For, what is preferred by the flesh, [is] death, whereas, what is preferred by the spirit, [is] life and peace;—
text.DRB: For the wisdom of the flesh is death; but the wisdom of the spirit is life and peace.
text.KJV: 8:6 For to be carnally minded, is death: but to be spiritually minded, is life and peace:
text.NABRE: 8:6 The concern of the flesh is death, but the concern of the spirit is life and peace.

text.NRSV: For this reason the mind that is set on the flesh is hostile to God; it does not submit to God’s law—indeed it cannot,
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as, what is preferred by the flesh, [is] hostile towards God, for, unto the law of God, it doth not submit itself, neither in fact can it.—
text.DRB: Because the wisdom of the flesh is an enemy to God; for it is not subject to the law of God, neither can it be.
text.KJV: 8:7 Because the carnall minde is enmitie against God: for it is not subiect to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
text.NABRE: 8:7 For the concern of the flesh is hostility toward God; it does not submit to the law of God, nor can it;

text.NRSV: and those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
text.Rotherham: They, moreover, who in flesh have their being, cannot please, God.
text.DRB: And they who are in the flesh, cannot please God.
text.KJV: 8:8 So then they that are in the flesh, cannot please God.
text.NABRE: 8:8 and those who are in the flesh cannot please God.

text.NRSV: But you are not in the flesh; you are in the Spirit, since the Spirit of God dwells in you. Anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, have not your being in flesh, but in spirit,—if at least, God’s Spirit, dwelleth in you; and, if anyone hath not Christ’s Spirit, the same, is not his;—
text.DRB: But you are not in the flesh, but in the spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
text.KJV: 8:9 But ye are not in the flesh, but in the spirit, if so be that the spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man haue not the spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
text.NABRE: 8:9 But you are not in the flesh; on the contrary, you are in the spirit, if only the Spirit of God dwells in you. Whoever does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.

text.NRSV: But if Christ is in you, though the body is dead because of sin, the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: But, if Christ is in you, the body, indeed, is dead by reason of sin, whereas, the spirit, is life by reason of righteousness;
text.DRB: And if Christ be in you, the body indeed is dead, because of sin; but the spirit liveth, because of justification.
text.KJV: 8:10 And if Christ in you, the body is dead because of sinne: but the spirit is life, because of righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: 8:10 But if Christ is in you, although the body is dead because of sin, the spirit is alive because of righteousness.

text.NRSV: If the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your mortal bodies also through his Spirit that dwells in you.
text.Rotherham: If, moreover, the Spirit of him that raised Jesus from among the dead dwelleth in you, he that raised from among the dead Christ Jesus, shall make alive [even] your death-doomed bodies, through means of his indwelling Spirit within you.
text.DRB: And if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead, dwell in you; he that raised up Jesus Christ from the dead, shall quicken also your mortal bodies, because of his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
text.KJV: 8:11 But if the spirit of him that raised vp Iesus from the dead, dwell in you: he that raised vp Christ from the dead, shall also quicken your mortall bodies, by his spirit that dwelleth in you.
text.NABRE: 8:11 If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your mortal bodies also, through his Spirit that dwells in you.

text.NRSV: So then, brothers and sisters, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh—
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, brethren—debtors, we are, not unto the flesh, that, according to flesh, we should live,—
text.DRB: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.
text.KJV: 8:12 Therfore brethren, we are detters, not to the flesh, to liue after the flesh.
text.NABRE: 8:12 Consequently, brothers, we are not debtors to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.

text.NRSV: for if you live according to the flesh, you will die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
text.Rotherham: For, if according to flesh ye live, ye are about to die, whereas, if in spirit, the practices of the flesh, ye are putting to death, ye shall attain unto life;
text.DRB: For if you live according to the flesh, you shall die: but if by the Spirit you mortify the deeds of the flesh, you shall live.
text.KJV: 8:13 For if ye liue after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the spirit doe mortifie the deeds of the body, ye shall liue.
text.NABRE: 8:13 For if you live according to the flesh, you will die, but if by the spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live. Children of God Through Adoption.

text.NRSV: For all who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God.
text.Rotherham: For, as many as by God’s Spirit are being led, the same, are God’s sons,—
text.DRB: For whosoever are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
text.KJV: 8:14 For as many as are led by the spirit of God, they are the sonnes of God.
text.NABRE: 8:14 For those who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God.

text.NRSV: For you did not receive a spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you have received a spirit of adoption. When we cry, “Abba! Father!”
text.Rotherham: For ye have not received a spirit of servitude, leading back into fear, but ye have received a spirit of sonship, whereby we are exclaiming—Abba! Oh Father!
text.DRB: For you have not received the spirit of bondage again in fear; but you have received the spirit of adoption of sons, whereby we cry: Abba (Father).
text.KJV: 8:15 For ye haue not receiued the spirit of bondage againe to feare: but ye haue receiued the spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, father.
text.NABRE: 8:15 For you did not receive a spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you received a spirit of adoption, through which we cry, “Abba, Father!”

text.NRSV: it is that very Spirit bearing witness with our spirit that we are children of God,
text.Rotherham: The Spirit itself, beareth witness together with our spirit that we are children of God;
text.DRB: For the Spirit himself giveth testimony to our spirit, that we are the sons of God.
text.KJV: 8:16 The spirit it selfe beareth witnes with our spirit, that we are the children of God.
text.NABRE: 8:16 The Spirit itself bears witness with our spirit that we are children of God,

text.NRSV: and if children, then heirs, heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ—if, in fact, we suffer with him so that we may also be glorified with him.
text.Rotherham: And, if children, heirs also—heirs, indeed, of God, but co-heirs with Christ,—if, at least, we are suffering together, in order that we may also be glorified together.
text.DRB: And if sons, heirs also; heirs indeed of God, and joint heirs with Christ: yet so, if we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified with him.
text.KJV: 8:17 And if children, then heires, heires of God, and ioynt heires with Christ: if so be that we suffer with him, that wee may be also glorified together.
text.NABRE: 8:17 and if children, then heirs, heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ, if only we suffer with him so that we may also be glorified with him. Destiny of Glory.

text.NRSV: I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory about to be revealed to us.
text.Rotherham: For I reckon that unworthy are the sufferings of the present season to be compared with the glory about to be revealed towards us;
text.DRB: For I reckon that the sufferings of this time are not worthy to be compared with the glory to come, that shall be revealed in us.
text.KJV: 8:18 For I reckon, that the sufferings of this present time, are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be reuealed in vs.
text.NABRE: 8:18 I consider that the sufferings of this present time are as nothing compared with the glory to be revealed for us.

text.NRSV: For the creation waits with eager longing for the revealing of the children of God;
text.Rotherham: For, the eager outlook of creation, ardently awaiteth the revealing of the sons of God,—
text.DRB: For the expectation of the creature waiteth for the revelation of the sons of God.
text.KJV: 8:19 For the earnest expectation of the creature, waiteth for the manifestation of the sonnes of God.
text.NABRE: 8:19 For creation awaits with eager expectation the revelation of the children of God;

text.NRSV: for the creation was subjected to futility, not of its own will but by the will of the one who subjected it, in hope
text.Rotherham: For, unto vanity, hath creation been made subject—not by choice, but by reason of him that made it subject, in hope
text.DRB: For the creature was made subject to vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him that made it subject, in hope:
text.KJV: 8:20 For the creature was made subiect to vanitie, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subiected the same in hope:
text.NABRE: 8:20 for creation was made subject to futility, not of its own accord but because of the one who subjected it, in hope

text.NRSV: that the creation itself will be set free from its bondage to decay and will obtain the freedom of the glory of the children of God.
text.Rotherham: That, creation itself also, shall be freed—from the bondage of the decay into the freedom of the glory of the sons of God;
text.DRB: Because the creature also itself shall be delivered from the servitude of corruption, into the liberty of the glory of the children of God.
text.KJV: 8:21 Because the creature it selfe also shall bee deliuered from the bondage of corruption, into the glorious libertie of the children of God.
text.NABRE: 8:21 that creation itself would be set free from slavery to corruption and share in the glorious freedom of the children of God.

text.NRSV: We know that the whole creation has been groaning in labor pains until now;
text.Rotherham: For we know that, all creation, is sighing together, and travailing-in-birth-throes together until the present,—
text.DRB: For we know that every creature groaneth and travaileth in pain, even till now.
text.KJV: 8:22 For wee know that the whole creation groaneth, and trauaileth in paine together vntill now.
text.NABRE: 8:22 We know that all creation is groaning in labor pains even until now;

text.NRSV: and not only the creation, but we ourselves, who have the first fruits of the Spirit, groan inwardly while we wait for adoption, the redemption of our bodies.
text.Rotherham: And, not only so, but, we ourselves, also, who have the first-fruit of the Spirit—[we] even ourselves, within our own selves do sigh,—sonship ardently awaiting—the redeeming of our body;—
text.DRB: And not only it, but ourselves also, who have the firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the adoption of the sons of God, the redemption of our body.
text.KJV: 8:23 And not only they, but our selues also which haue the first fruites of the spirit, euen we our selues groane within our selues, waiting for the adoption, to wit, the redemption of our body.
text.NABRE: 8:23 and not only that, but we ourselves, who have the firstfruits of the Spirit, we also groan within ourselves as we wait for adoption, the redemption of our bodies.

text.NRSV: For in hope we were saved. Now hope that is seen is not hope. For who hopes for what is seen?
text.Rotherham: For, by our hope, have we been saved,—but, hope beheld, is not hope, for, what one beholdeth, why doth he hope for?
text.DRB: For we are saved by hope. But hope that is seen, is not hope. For what a man seeth, why doth he hope for?
text.KJV: 8:24 For wee are saued by hope: but hope that is seene, is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
text.NABRE: 8:24 For in hope we were saved. Now hope that sees for itself is not hope. For who hopes for what one sees?

text.NRSV: But if we hope for what we do not see, we wait for it with patience.
text.Rotherham: If, however, what we do not behold we hope for, with endurance, are we ardently awaiting [it] ;—
text.DRB: But if we hope for that which we see not, we wait for it with patience.
text.KJV: 8:25 But if wee hope for that wee see not, then doe wee with patience waite for it.
text.NABRE: 8:25 But if we hope for what we do not see, we wait with endurance.

text.NRSV: Likewise the Spirit helps us in our weakness; for we do not know how to pray as we ought, but that very Spirit intercedes with sighs too deep for words.
text.Rotherham: In the selfsame way moreover, even the Spirit, helpeth together in our weakness,—for, what we should pray for as we ought, we know not, but, the Spirit itself, maketh intercession with sighings unutterable,
text.DRB: Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmity. For we know not what we should pray for as we ought; but the Spirit himself asketh for us with unspeakable groanings.
text.KJV: 8:26 Likewise the spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what wee should pray for as wee ought: but the spirit it selfe maketh intercession for vs with groanings, which cannot bee vttered.
text.NABRE: 8:26 In the same way, the Spirit too comes to the aid of our weakness; for we do not know how to pray as we ought, but the Spirit itself intercedes with inexpressible groanings.

text.NRSV: And God, who searches the heart, knows what is the mind of the Spirit, because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to the will of God.
text.Rotherham: And, he that searcheth the hearts, knoweth what is preferred by the Spirit—that, according to God, he maketh intercession in behalf of saints;
text.DRB: And he that searcheth the hearts, knoweth what the Spirit desireth; because he asketh for the saints according to God.
text.KJV: 8:27 And he that searcheth the hearts, knoweth what is the minde of the spirit, because he maketh intercession for the Saints, according to the will of God.
text.NABRE: 8:27 And the one who searches hearts knows what is the intention of the Spirit, because it intercedes for the holy ones according to God’s will.

text.NRSV: We know that all things work together for good for those who love God, who are called according to his purpose.
text.Rotherham: We know, further, that, unto them who love God, God causeth all things to work together for good,—unto them who, according to purpose, are such as he hath called;
text.DRB: And we know that to them that love God, all things work together unto good, to such as, according to his purpose, are called to be saints.
text.KJV: 8:28 And wee know that all things worke together for good, to them that loue God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
text.NABRE: 8:28 God’s Indomitable Love in Christ. We know that all things work for good for those who love God, who are called according to his purpose.

text.NRSV: For those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, in order that he might be the firstborn within a large family.
text.Rotherham: For, whom he fore-approved, he also fore-appointed to be conformed unto the image of his Son, that he might be firstborn among many brethren,—
text.DRB: For whom he foreknew, he also predestinated to be made conformable to the image of his Son; that he might be the firstborn amongst many brethren.
text.KJV: 8:29 For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his sonne, that hee might bee the first borne amongst many brethren.
text.NABRE: 8:29 For those he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, so that he might be the firstborn among many brothers.

text.NRSV: And those whom he predestined he also called; and those whom he called he also justified; and those whom he justified he also glorified.
text.Rotherham: And, whom he fore-appointed, the same, he also called, and, whom he called, the same, he also declared righteous, and, whom he declared righteous, the same, he also made glorious:—
text.DRB: And whom he predestinated, them he also called. And whom he called, them he also justified. And whom he justified, them he also glorified.
text.KJV: 8:30 Moreouer, whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also iustified: and whom he iustified, them he also glorified.
text.NABRE: 8:30 And those he predestined he also called; and those he called he also justified; and those he justified he also glorified.

text.NRSV: What then are we to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall we say to a these things? If God [is] for us, who [shall be] against us?
text.DRB: What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who is against us?
text.KJV: 8:31 What shall wee then say to these things? If God be for vs, who can bee against vs?
text.NABRE: 8:31 What then shall we say to this? If God is for us, who can be against us?

text.NRSV: He who did not withhold his own Son, but gave him up for all of us, will he not with him also give us everything else?
text.Rotherham: He, at least, who his own Son did not spare, but in behalf of us all delivered him up, how shall he not also, with him, all things upon us in favour bestow?
text.DRB: He that spared not even his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how hath he not also, with him, given us all things?
text.KJV: 8:32 He that spared not his owne son, but deliuered him vp for vs all: how shall hee not with him also freely giue vs all things?
text.NABRE: 8:32 He who did not spare his own Son but handed him over for us all, how will he not also give us everything else along with him?

text.NRSV: Who will bring any charge against God’s elect? It is God who justifies.
text.Rotherham: Who shall bring an accusation against the chosen ones of God? God, who declareth righteous?
text.DRB: Who shall accuse against the elect of God? God that justifieth.
text.KJV: 8:33 Who shall lay any thing to the charge of Gods elect? It is God that iustifieth:
text.NABRE: 8:33 Who will bring a charge against God’s chosen ones? It is God who acquits us.

text.NRSV: Who is to condemn? It is Christ Jesus, who died, yes, who was raised, who is at the right hand of God, who indeed intercedes for us.
text.Rotherham: Who is he that condemneth? Christ [Jesus] who died?—Nay! rather was raised [from among the dead],—who is on the right hand of God, who also is making intercession in our behalf?
text.DRB: Who is he that shall condemn? Christ Jesus that died, yea that is risen also again; who is at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
text.KJV: 8:34 Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died, yea rather that is risen againe, who is euen at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for vs.
text.NABRE: 8:34 Who will condemn? It is Christ [Jesus] who died, rather, was raised, who also is at the right hand of God, who indeed intercedes for us.

text.NRSV: Who will separate us from the love of Christ? Will hardship, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
text.Rotherham: Who shall separate us from the love of the Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?—
text.DRB: Who then shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation? or distress? or famine? or nakedness? or danger? or persecution? or the sword?
text.KJV: 8:35 Who shall separate vs from the loue of Christ? shall tribulation, or distresse, or persecution, or famine, or nakednesse, or perill, or sword?
text.NABRE: 8:35 What will separate us from the love of Christ? Will anguish, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or the sword?

text.NRSV: As it is written, “For your sake we are being killed all day long; we are accounted as sheep to be slaughtered.”
text.Rotherham: According as it is written—For thy sake, are we being put to death all the day long, we have been reckoned as sheep for slaughter.—
text.DRB: (As it is written: For thy sake we are put to death all the day long. We are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.)
text.KJV: 8:36 (As it is written, for thy sake we are killed all the day long, wee are accounted as sheepe for the slaughter.)
text.NABRE: 8:36 As it is written: “For your sake we are being slain all the day; we are looked upon as sheep to be slaughtered.”

text.NRSV: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.
text.Rotherham: Nay, in all these things, we are more than conquering through him that hath loved us.
text.DRB: But in all these things we overcome, because of him that hath loved us.
text.KJV: 8:37 Nay in all these things wee are more then conquerours, through him that loued vs.
text.NABRE: 8:37 No, in all these things we conquer overwhelmingly through him who loved us.

text.NRSV: For I am convinced that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor rulers, nor things present, nor things to come, nor powers,
text.Rotherham: For I am persuaded that—neither death nor life, nor messengers nor principalities, nor things present nor things to come, nor powers,
text.DRB: For I am sure that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come, nor might,
text.KJV: 8:38 For I am perswaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
text.NABRE: 8:38 For I am convinced that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor present things, nor future things, nor powers,

text.NRSV: nor height, nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Nor height nor depth, nor any other created thing, shall be able to separate us from the love of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.DRB: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: 8:39 Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shalbe able to separate vs from the loue of God, which is in Christ Iesus our Lord.
text.NABRE: 8:39 nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: I am speaking the truth in Christ—I am not lying; my conscience confirms it by the Holy Spirit—
text.Rotherham: Truth, say I, in Christ, I utter no falsehood,—my conscience bearing witness with me in the Holy Spirit,—
text.DRB: I speak the truth in Christ, I lie not, my conscience bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost:
text.KJV: 9:1 I say the trueth in Christ, I lie not, my conscience also bearing mee witnesse in the holy Ghost,
text.NABRE: 9:1 V. Jews and Gentiles in God’s Plan Chapter 9 Paul’s Love for Israel. I speak the truth in Christ, I do not lie; my conscience joins with the holy Spirit in bearing me witness

text.NRSV: I have great sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart.
text.Rotherham: That I have great grief and incessant travail in my heart;
text.DRB: That I have great sadness, and continual sorrow in my heart.
text.KJV: 9:2 That I haue great heauinesse, and continuall sorrow in my heart.
text.NABRE: 9:2 that I have great sorrow and constant anguish in my heart.

text.NRSV: For I could wish that I myself were accursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my own people, my kindred according to the flesh.
text.Rotherham: For I could have wished to be accursed, even I myself, from the Christ, in behalf of my brethren my kinsmen according to the flesh;—
text.DRB: For I wished myself to be an anathema from Christ, for my brethren, who are my kinsmen according to the flesh,
text.KJV: 9:3 For I could wish that my selfe were accursed from Christ, for my brethren my kinsemen according to the flesh:
text.NABRE: 9:3 For I could wish that I myself were accursed and separated from Christ for the sake of my brothers, my kin according to the flesh.

text.NRSV: They are Israelites, and to them belong the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the worship, and the promises;
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, are Israelites, whose are the sonship, and the glory, and the covenants, and the legislation, and the divine service, and the promises,
text.DRB: Who are Israelites, to whom belongeth the adoption as of children, and the glory, and the testament, and the giving of the law, and the service of God, and the promises:
text.KJV: 9:4 Who are Israelites: to whom perteineth the adoption, and the glory, and the couenants, and the giuing of the Law, and the seruice of God, and the promises:
text.NABRE: 9:4 They are Israelites; theirs the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the worship, and the promises;

text.NRSV: to them belong the patriarchs, and from them, according to the flesh, comes the Messiah, who is over all, God blessed forever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Whose are the fathers, and of whom is the Christ—according to the flesh—he who is over all, God, blessed unto the ages. Amen.
text.DRB: Whose are the fathers, and of whom is Christ, according to the flesh, who is over all things, God blessed for ever. Amen.
text.KJV: 9:5 Whose are the fathers, and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came, who is ouer all, God blessed for euer, Amen.
text.NABRE: 9:5 theirs the patriarchs, and from them, according to the flesh, is the Messiah. God who is over all be blessed forever. Amen.

text.NRSV: It is not as though the word of God had failed. For not all Israelites truly belong to Israel,
text.Rotherham: It is not, however, as though the word of God had failed; for, not all they who are of Israel, the same are Israel.
text.DRB: Not as though the word of God hath miscarried. For all are not Israelites that are of Israel:
text.KJV: 9:6 Not as though the word of God hath taken none effect. For they are not all Israel which are of Israel:
text.NABRE: 9:6 God’s Free Choice. But it is not that the word of God has failed. For not all who are of Israel are Israel,

text.NRSV: and not all of Abraham’s children are his true descendants; but “It is through Isaac that descendants shall be named for you.”
text.Rotherham: Neither, because they are seed of Abraham, are all children,—but, In Isaac, shall there be called unto thee a seed.
text.DRB: Neither are all they that are the seed of Abraham, children; but in Isaac shall thy seed be called:
text.KJV: 9:7 Neither because they are the seed of Abraham are they all children: but in Isaac shall thy seed be called.
text.NABRE: 9:7 nor are they all children of Abraham because they are his descendants; but “It is through Isaac that descendants shall bear your name.”

text.NRSV: This means that it is not the children of the flesh who are the children of God, but the children of the promise are counted as descendants.
text.Rotherham: That is—not the children of the flesh, the same are children of God; but, the children of the promise, are reckoned as a seed.
text.DRB: That is to say, not they that are the children of the flesh, are the children of God; but they, that are the children of the promise, are accounted for the seed.
text.KJV: 9:8 That is, They which are the children of the flesh, these are not the children of God: but the children of the promise are counted for the seed.
text.NABRE: 9:8 This means that it is not the children of the flesh who are the children of God, but the children of the promise are counted as descendants.

text.NRSV: For this is what the promise said, “About this time I will return and Sarah shall have a son.”
text.Rotherham: For, of promise, is this word—According to this season, will I come, and Sarah shall have a son.
text.DRB: For this is the word of promise: According to this time will I come; and Sara shall have a son.
text.KJV: 9:9 For this is the word of promise, At this time will I come, and Sara shall haue a sonne.
text.NABRE: 9:9 For this is the wording of the promise, “About this time I shall return and Sarah will have a son.”

text.NRSV: Nor is that all; something similar happened to Rebecca when she had conceived children by one husband, our ancestor Isaac.
text.Rotherham: And, not only so, but, when, Rebekah also, was with child, of one—Isaac our father,—
text.DRB: And not only she. But when Rebecca also had conceived at once, of Isaac our father.
text.KJV: 9:10 And not onely this, but when Rebecca also had conceiued by one, euen by our father Isaac,
text.NABRE: 9:10 And not only that, but also when Rebecca had conceived children by one husband, our father Isaac —

text.NRSV: Even before they had been born or had done anything good or bad (so that God’s purpose of election might continue,
text.Rotherham: They, in fact, not being yet born, nor having practised anything good or bad,—in order that the purpose of God by way of election might stand,—not by works but by him that was calling,
text.DRB: For when the children were not yet born , nor had done any good or evil (that the purpose of God, according to election, might stand,)
text.KJV: 9:11 (For the children being not yet borne, neither hauing done any good or euil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, not of workes, but of him that calleth.)
text.NABRE: 9:11 before they had yet been born or had done anything, good or bad, in order that God’s elective plan might continue,

text.NRSV: not by works but by his call) she was told, “The elder shall serve the younger.”
text.Rotherham: It was said unto her—The elder, shall serve the younger;
text.DRB: Not of works, but of him that calleth, it was said to her: The elder shall serve the younger.
text.KJV: 9:12 It was said vnto her, The elder shall serue the yonger.
text.NABRE: 9:12 not by works but by his call—she was told, “The older shall serve the younger.”

text.NRSV: As it is written, “I have loved Jacob, but I have hated Esau.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—Jacob, have I loved, but, Esau, have I hated.
text.DRB: As it is written: Jacob I have loved, but Esau I have hated.
text.KJV: 9:13 As it is written, Iacob haue I loued, but Esau haue I hated.
text.NABRE: 9:13 As it is written: “I loved Jacob but hated Esau.”

text.NRSV: What then are we to say? Is there injustice on God’s part? By no means!
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall we say? Is there injustice with God? Far be it!
text.DRB: What shall we say then? Is there injustice with God? God forbid.
text.KJV: 9:14 What shall we say then? Is there vnrighteousnes with God? God forbid.
text.NABRE: 9:14 What then are we to say? Is there injustice on the part of God? Of course not!

text.NRSV: For he says to Moses, “I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion.”
text.Rotherham: For, unto Moses, he saith—I will have mercy upon whomsoever I can have mercy, and I will have compassion upon whomsoever I can have compassion.
text.DRB: For he saith to Moses: I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy; and I will shew mercy to whom I will shew mercy.
text.KJV: 9:15 For hee saith to Moses, I will haue mercy on whom I wil haue mercie, and I will haue compassion on whom I will haue compassion.
text.NABRE: 9:15 For he says to Moses: “I will show mercy to whom I will, I will take pity on whom I will.”

text.NRSV: So it depends not on human will or exertion, but on God who shows mercy.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, it is nor of him that wisheth nor of him that runneth, but of the mercy-shewing God.
text.DRB: So then it is not of him that willeth , nor of him that runneth, but of God that sheweth mercy.
text.KJV: 9:16 So then it is not of him that willeth, nor of him that runneth, but of God that sheweth mercy.
text.NABRE: 9:16 So it depends not upon a person’s will or exertion, but upon God, who shows mercy.

text.NRSV: For the scripture says to Pharaoh, “I have raised you up for the very purpose of showing my power in you, so that my name may be proclaimed in all the earth.”
