text.KJV: 14:10 But why doest thou iudge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? wee shall all stand before the Iudgement seat of Christ.
text.NABRE: 14:10 Why then do you judge your brother? Or you, why do you look down on your brother? For we shall all stand before the judgment seat of God;

text.NRSV: For it is written, “As I live, says the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall give praise to God.”
text.Rotherham: For it is written—Living am, I, saith the Lord, unto me, shall bow every knee, and, every tongue, shall openly confess unto God.
text.DRB: For it is written: As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.
text.KJV: 14:11 For it is written, As I liue, saith the Lord, euery knee shall bow to mee, and euery tongue shall confesse to God.
text.NABRE: 14:11 for it is written: “As I live, says the Lord, every knee shall bend before me, and every tongue shall give praise to God.”

text.NRSV: So then, each of us will be accountable to God.
text.Rotherham: Hence, [then], each one of us, of himself shall give account unto God.
text.DRB: Therefore every one of us shall render account to God for himself.
text.KJV: 14:12 So then euery one of vs shall giue accompt of himselfe to God.
text.NABRE: 14:12 So [then] each of us shall give an account of himself [to God].

text.NRSV: Let us therefore no longer pass judgment on one another, but resolve instead never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of another.
text.Rotherham: No longer, then, upon one another, let us be sitting in judgment, but, this, judge ye rather—not to be putting a cause of stumbling before your brother or an occasion to fall.
text.DRB: Let us not therefore judge one another any more. But judge this rather, that you put not a stumblingblock or a scandal in your brother's way.
text.KJV: 14:13 Let vs not therefore iudge one another any more: but iudge this rather, that no man put a stumbling blocke, or an occasion to fall in his brothers way.
text.NABRE: 14:13 Consideration for the Weak Conscience. Then let us no longer judge one another, but rather resolve never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of a brother.

text.NRSV: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself; but it is unclean for anyone who thinks it unclean.
text.Rotherham: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus—that, nothing, is profane of itself,—save to him who reckoneth anything to be profane, unto that man, [it is] profane,
text.DRB: I know, and am confident in the Lord Jesus, that nothing is unclean of itself; but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.
text.KJV: 14:14 I know, and am perswaded by the Lord Iesus, that there is nothing vncleane of it selfe: but to him that esteemeth any thing to bee vncleane, to him it is vncleane.
text.NABRE: 14:14 I know and am convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself; still, it is unclean for someone who thinks it unclean.

text.NRSV: If your brother or sister is being injured by what you eat, you are no longer walking in love. Do not let what you eat cause the ruin of one for whom Christ died.
text.Rotherham: If, in fact, because of food, thy brother is being grieved, no longer, by the rule of love, art thou walking:—do not, by thy food, that man, be destroying, on whose behalf Christ died!
text.DRB: For if, because of thy meat, thy brother be grieved, thou walkest not now according to charity. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.
text.KJV: 14:15 But if thy brother be grieued with thy meate: now walkest thou not charitably. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.
text.NABRE: 14:15 If your brother is being hurt by what you eat, your conduct is no longer in accord with love. Do not because of your food destroy him for whom Christ died.

text.NRSV: So do not let your good be spoken of as evil.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, suffer not to be defamed, your own good thing;
text.DRB: Let not then our good be evil spoken of.
text.KJV: 14:16 Let not then your good be euill spoken of.
text.NABRE: 14:16 So do not let your good be reviled.

text.NRSV: For the kingdom of God is not food and drink but righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For the kingdom of God is not eating and drinking, but righteousness and peace and joy in Holy Spirit;
text.DRB: For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but justice, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
text.KJV: 14:17 For the kingdome of God is not meat and drinke; but righteousnes, and peace, and ioy in the holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 14:17 For the kingdom of God is not a matter of food and drink, but of righteousness, peace, and joy in the holy Spirit;

text.NRSV: The one who thus serves Christ is acceptable to God and has human approval.
text.Rotherham: For, he that in this doeth service unto the Christ, is acceptable unto God, and approved unto men.
text.DRB: For he that in this serveth Christ, pleaseth God, and is approved of men.
text.KJV: 14:18 For hee that in these things serueth Christ, is acceptable to God, and approued of men.
text.NABRE: 14:18 whoever serves Christ in this way is pleasing to God and approved by others.

text.NRSV: Let us then pursue what makes for peace and for mutual upbuilding.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, the things pertaining to peace, let us pursue, and the things which belong to the upbuilding one of another:
text.DRB: Therefore let us follow after the things that are of peace; and keep the things that are of edification one towards another.
text.KJV: 14:19 Let vs therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edifie an other.
text.NABRE: 14:19 Let us then pursue what leads to peace and to building up one another.

text.NRSV: Do not, for the sake of food, destroy the work of God. Everything is indeed clean, but it is wrong for you to make others fall by what you eat;
text.Rotherham: Do not, for the sake of food, be throwing down the work of God! All things, indeed, are pure; but, ill, is it for the man who with occasion of stumbling doth eat,—
text.DRB: Destroy not the work of God for meat. All things indeed are clean: but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.
text.KJV: 14:20 For meat, destroy not the worke of God: all things indeed are pure; but it is euill for that man who eateth with offence.
text.NABRE: 14:20 For the sake of food, do not destroy the work of God. Everything is indeed clean, but it is wrong for anyone to become a stumbling block by eating;

text.NRSV: it is good not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything that makes your brother or sister stumble.
text.Rotherham: Well, is it not to eat flesh nor to drink wine nor [to do aught] whereby thy brother is caused to stumble.
text.DRB: It is good not to eat flesh, and not to drink wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother is offended, or scandalized, or made weak.
text.KJV: 14:21 It is good neither to eate flesh, nor to drinke wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is offended, or is made weake.
text.NABRE: 14:21 it is good not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything that causes your brother to stumble.

text.NRSV: The faith that you have, have as your own conviction before God. Blessed are those who have no reason to condemn themselves because of what they approve.
text.Rotherham: The faith which thou hast, have to thyself before God: happy, he that bringeth not judgment upon himself by that which he approveth;
text.DRB: Hast thou faith? Have it to thyself before God. Blessed is he that condemneth not himself in that which he alloweth.
text.KJV: 14:22 Hast thou faith? haue it to thy selfe before God. Happie is he that condemneth not himselfe in that thing which hee alloweth.
text.NABRE: 14:22 Keep the faith [that] you have to yourself in the presence of God; blessed is the one who does not condemn himself for what he approves.

text.NRSV: But those who have doubts are condemned if they eat, because they do not eat from faith; for whatever does not proceed from faith is sin.
text.Rotherham: But, he that is in doubt, if he eat, hath condemned himself,—because, [it was] not of faith, and, everything which is not of faith, is sin.
text.DRB: But he that discerneth , if he eat, is condemned; because not of faith . For all that is not of faith is sin.
text.KJV: 14:23 And hee that doubteth, is damned if hee eate, because hee eateth not of faith: For whatsoeuer is not of faith, is sinne.
text.NABRE: 14:23 But whoever has doubts is condemned if he eats, because this is not from faith; for whatever is not from faith is sin.

text.NRSV: We who are strong ought to put up with the failings of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
text.Rotherham: We are bound, however, we, who are strong, the weakness of them who are not strong to be bearing, and not, unto ourselves, to give pleasure.
text.DRB: Now we that are stronger, ought to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
text.KJV: 15:1 Wee then that are strong, ought to beare the infirmities of the weake, and not to please our selues.
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 Patience and Self-Denial. We who are strong ought to put up with the failings of the weak and not to please ourselves;

text.NRSV: Each of us must please our neighbor for the good purpose of building up the neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Let, each one of us, unto his neighbour give pleasure—for what is good unto upbuilding;
text.DRB: Let every one of you please his neighbour unto good, to edification.
text.KJV: 15:2 Let euery one of vs please his neighbour for his good to edification.
text.NABRE: 15:2 let each of us please our neighbor for the good, for building up.

text.NRSV: For Christ did not please himself; but as it is written, “The insults of those who insult you have fallen on me.”
text.Rotherham: For, even the Christ, not unto himself gave pleasure, but, even as it is written—The reproaches of them that we reproaching thee, fell upon me:
text.DRB: For Christ did not please himself, but as it is written: The reproaches of them that reproached thee, fell upon me.
text.KJV: 15:3 For euen Christ pleased not himselfe, but as it is written, The reproches of them that reproched thee, fell on mee.
text.NABRE: 15:3 For Christ did not please himself; but, as it is written, “The insults of those who insult you fall upon me.”

text.NRSV: For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, so that by steadfastness and by the encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever things, in fact, were written aforetime, [all] for our own instruction were written,—in order that, through endurance and through the encouragement of the Scriptures, we might have their hope.
text.DRB: For what things soever were written, were written for our learning: that through patience and the comfort of the scriptures, we might have hope.
text.KJV: 15:4 For whatsoeuer things were written aforetime, were written for our learning, that we through patience and comfort of the Scriptures might haue hope.
text.NABRE: 15:4 For whatever was written previously was written for our instruction, that by endurance and by the encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope.

text.NRSV: May the God of steadfastness and encouragement grant you to live in such harmony with one another, in accord with Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: Now may, the God of the endurance and of the encouragement, give you, the same thing, to be regarding amongst one another, according to Christ Jesus;
text.DRB: Now the God of patience and of comfort grant you to be of one mind one towards another, according to Jesus Christ:
text.KJV: 15:5 Now the God of patience and consolation graunt you to be like minded one towards another, according to Christ Iesus:
text.NABRE: 15:5 May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you to think in harmony with one another, in keeping with Christ Jesus,

text.NRSV: so that together you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: In order that, with one accord, with one mouth, ye maybe glorifying the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: That with one mind, and with one mouth, you may glorify God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 15:6 That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorifie God, euen the Father of our Lord Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 15:6 that with one accord you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God’s Fidelity and Mercy.

text.NRSV: Welcome one another, therefore, just as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore receive ye one another, even as, Christ also, hath received us unto the glory of God:—
text.DRB: Wherefore receive one another, as Christ also hath received you unto the honour of God.
text.KJV: 15:7 Wherfore receiue yee one another, as Christ also receiued vs, to the glory of God.
text.NABRE: 15:7 Welcome one another, then, as Christ welcomed you, for the glory of God.

text.NRSV: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant of the circumcised on behalf of the truth of God in order that he might confirm the promises given to the patriarchs,
text.Rotherham: For I affirm Christ to have become a minister of circumcision in behalf of the truth of God,—to confirm the promises of the fathers,
text.DRB: For I say that Christ Jesus was minister of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers.
text.KJV: 15:8 Now I say, that Iesus Christ was a Minister of the circumcision for the trueth of God, to confirme the promises made vnto the fathers:
text.NABRE: 15:8 For I say that Christ became a minister of the circumcised to show God’s truthfulness, to confirm the promises to the patriarchs,

text.NRSV: and in order that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Therefore I will confess you among the Gentiles, and sing praises to your name”;
text.Rotherham: And that, the nations, for mercy should glorify God:—even as it is written—For this cause, will I openly confess unto thee among nations, and, unto thy name, will strike the strings;
text.DRB: But that the Gentiles are to glorify God for his mercy, as it is written: Therefore will I confess to thee, O Lord, among the Gentiles, and will sing to thy name.
text.KJV: 15:9 And that the Gentiles might glorifie God for his mercie, as it is written, For this cause I will confesse to thee among the Gentiles, and sing vnto thy Name.
text.NABRE: 15:9 but so that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written: “Therefore, I will praise you among the Gentiles and sing praises to your name.”

text.NRSV: and again he says, “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people”;
text.Rotherham: And again he saith—Be glad ye nations with his people;
text.DRB: And again he saith: Rejoice, ye Gentiles, with his people.
text.KJV: 15:10 And againe he saith, Reioyce yee Gentiles with his people.
text.NABRE: 15:10 And again it says: “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people.”

text.NRSV: and again, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples praise him”;
text.Rotherham: And again,—Be giving praise all ye nations unto the Lord, and let all the peoples repeat his praise;
text.DRB: And again: Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and magnify him, all ye people.
text.KJV: 15:11 And againe, Praise the Lord all ye Gentiles, and laud him all ye people.
text.NABRE: 15:11 And again: “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples praise him.”

text.NRSV: and again Isaiah says, “The root of Jesse shall come, the one who rises to rule the Gentiles; in him the Gentiles shall hope.”
text.Rotherham: And, again, Isaiah, saith—There shall be the root of Jesse, and he that ariseth to rule nations,—upon, him, shall nations hope.
text.DRB: And again Isaias saith: There shall be a root of Jesse; and he that shall rise up to rule the Gentiles, in him the Gentiles shall hope.
text.KJV: 15:12 And againe Esaias saith, There shal be a roote of Iesse, and he that shal rise to raigne ouer the Gentiles, in him shall the Gentiles trust.
text.NABRE: 15:12 And again Isaiah says: “The root of Jesse shall come, raised up to rule the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles hope.”

text.NRSV: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you may abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Now, the God of the hope, fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that ye may surpass in the hope, in the power of Holy Spirit.
text.DRB: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing; that you may abound in hope, and in the power of the Holy Ghost.
text.KJV: 15:13 Nowe the God of hope fill you with all ioy and peace in beleeuing, that yee may abound in hope through the power of the holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 15:13 May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you may abound in hope by the power of the holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: I myself feel confident about you, my brothers and sisters, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and able to instruct one another.
text.Rotherham: But I am persuaded my brethren,—even, I myself, concerning you: that, ye yourselves, also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also, unto one another, to be ministering admonition:
text.DRB: And I myself also, my brethren, am assured of you, that you also are full of love, replenished with all knowledge, so that you are able to admonish one another.
text.KJV: 15:14 And I my selfe also am perswaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodnesse, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
text.NABRE: 15:14 VII. Conclusion Apostle to the Gentiles. I myself am convinced about you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and able to admonish one another.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless on some points I have written to you rather boldly by way of reminder, because of the grace given me by God
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the more boldly, have I written unto you,—in some measure, as bringing it back to your minds,—by reason of the favour given unto me from God,—
text.DRB: But I have written to you, brethren, more boldly in some sort, as it were putting you in mind: because of the grace which is given me from God.
text.KJV: 15:15 Neuerthelesse, brethren, I haue written the more boldly vnto you, in some sort, as putting you in mind, because of the grace that is giuen to mee of God,
text.NABRE: 15:15 But I have written to you rather boldly in some respects to remind you, because of the grace given me by God

text.NRSV: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the offering of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: That I should be a public minister of Christ Jesus unto the nations, doing priestly service with the glad-message of God, in order that the offering up of the nations might prove to be acceptable, being hallowed in Holy Spirit:
text.DRB: That I should be the minister of Christ Jesus among the Gentiles; sanctifying the gospel of God, that the oblation of the Gentiles may be made acceptable and sanctified in the Holy Ghost.
text.KJV: 15:16 That I should be the minister of Iesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministring the Gospel of God, that the offering vp of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being sanctified by the holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 15:16 to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in performing the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the offering up of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: In Christ Jesus, then, I have reason to boast of my work for God.
text.Rotherham: I have, therefore, [my] boasting in Christ Jesus in the things pertaining to God,—
text.DRB: I have therefore glory in Christ Jesus towards God.
text.KJV: 15:17 I haue therfore whereof I may glory through Iesus Christ, in those things which pertaine to God.
text.NABRE: 15:17 In Christ Jesus, then, I have reason to boast in what pertains to God.

text.NRSV: For I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to win obedience from the Gentiles, by word and deed,
text.Rotherham: For I wilt not dare to speak anything save of the things which Christ hath wrought out for himself through me for the obedience of nations,—by word and deed,
text.DRB: For I dare not to speak of any of those things which Christ worketh not by me, for the obedience of the Gentiles, by word and deed,
text.KJV: 15:18 For I will not dare to speake of any of those things, which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deede,
text.NABRE: 15:18 For I will not dare to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to lead the Gentiles to obedience by word and deed,

text.NRSV: by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God, so that from Jerusalem and as far around as Illyricum I have fully proclaimed the good news of Christ.
text.Rotherham: By the power of signs and wonders, by the power of [Holy Spirit]; so that I, from Jerusalem and in a circuit as far as Illyricum, have fulfilled the glad-message of the Christ,—
text.DRB: By the virtue of signs and wonders, in the power of the Holy Ghost, so that from Jerusalem round about as far as unto Illyricum, I have replenished the gospel of Christ.
text.KJV: 15:19 Through mighty signes and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God, so that from Hierusalem and round about vnto Illyricum, I haue fully preached the Gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: 15:19 by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit [of God], so that from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum I have finished preaching the gospel of Christ.

text.NRSV: Thus I make it my ambition to proclaim the good news, not where Christ has already been named, so that I do not build on someone else’s foundation,
text.Rotherham: Although, thus,—as ambitious to be announcing the glad-message—not where Christ had been named, lest, upon another’s foundation, I should be building;
text.DRB: And I have so preached this gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man's foundation.
text.KJV: 15:20 Yea, so haue I striued to preach the Gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build vpon another mans foundation:
text.NABRE: 15:20 Thus I aspire to proclaim the gospel not where Christ has already been named, so that I do not build on another’s foundation,

text.NRSV: but as it is written, “Those who have never been told of him shall see, and those who have never heard shall understand.”
text.Rotherham: But, even as it is written—They shall see unto whom had been announced no tidings concerning him, and, they who had not heard, shall understand.
text.DRB: But as it is written: They to whom he was not spoken of, shall see, and they that have not heard shall understand.
text.KJV: 15:21 But as it is written, To whom hee was not spoken of, they shall see: and they that haue not heard, shall vnderstand.
text.NABRE: 15:21 but as it is written: “Those who have never been told of him shall see, and those who have never heard of him shall understand.”

text.NRSV: This is the reason that I have so often been hindered from coming to you.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, also, I have been hindered these many times from coming unto you;
text.DRB: For which cause also I was hindered very much from coming to you, and have been kept away till now.
text.KJV: 15:22 For which cause also I haue been much hindered from coming to you.
text.NABRE: 15:22 Paul’s Plans; Need for Prayers. That is why I have so often been prevented from coming to you.

text.NRSV: But now, with no further place for me in these regions, I have also for many years had a desire to come to you.
text.Rotherham: But, now, no longer having place in these regions, and having had, a longing, to come unto you a good many years,—
text.DRB: But now having no more place in these countries, and having a great desire these many years past to come unto you,
text.KJV: 15:23 But now hauing no more place in these parts, and hauing a great desire these many yeeres to come vnto you:
text.NABRE: 15:23 But now, since I no longer have any opportunity in these regions and since I have desired to come to you for many years,

text.NRSV: I hope to see you in passing as I go to Spain, and to be sent on my way there by you, once I have enjoyed your company for a little while.
text.Rotherham: As soon as I am journey to Spain,—I am hoping, in fact, when journeying through to get sight of you, and by you to be set forward thither,—if, with your company, I have first in some measure been satisfied.
text.DRB: When I shall begin to take my journey into Spain, I hope that as I pass, I shall see you, and be brought on my way thither by you, if first, in part, I shall have enjoyed you:
text.KJV: 15:24 Whensoeuer I take my iourney into Spaine, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my iourney, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your company.
text.NABRE: 15:24 I hope to see you in passing as I go to Spain and to be sent on my way there by you, after I have enjoyed being with you for a time.

text.NRSV: At present, however, I am going to Jerusalem in a ministry to the saints;
text.Rotherham: But, now, I am journeying unto Jerusalem, ministering unto the saints;
text.DRB: But now I shall go to Jerusalem, to minister unto the saints.
text.KJV: 15:25 But now I goe vnto Hierusalem, to minister vnto the Saints.
text.NABRE: 15:25 Now, however, I am going to Jerusalem to minister to the holy ones.

text.NRSV: for Macedonia and Achaia have been pleased to share their resources with the poor among the saints at Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: For Macedonia and Achaia have been well-pleased to make a certain fellowship for the destitute of the saints who are in Jerusalem,—
text.DRB: For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a contribution for the poor of the saints that are in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 15:26 For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia, to make a certaine contribution for the poore Saints which are at Hierusalem.
text.NABRE: 15:26 For Macedonia and Achaia have decided to make some contribution for the poor among the holy ones in Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: They were pleased to do this, and indeed they owe it to them; for if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual blessings, they ought also to be of service to them in material blessings.
text.Rotherham: They have been well-pleased, indeed,—and, their debtors, they are; for, if, with their spiritual things the nations have come into fellowship, they are bound also with their own carnal things to minister publicly unto them.
text.DRB: For it hath pleased them; and they are their debtors. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, they ought also in carnal things to minister to them.
text.KJV: 15:27 It hath pleased them verely, and their detters they are. For if the Gentiles haue bene made partakers of their spirituall things, their duetie is also to minister vnto them in carnall things.
text.NABRE: 15:27 they decided to do it, and in fact they are indebted to them, for if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual blessings, they ought also to serve them in material blessings.

text.NRSV: So, when I have completed this, and have delivered to them what has been collected, I will set out by way of you to Spain;
text.Rotherham: This, then, having completed, and sealed unto them this fruit, I will come back by you unto Spain:—
text.DRB: When therefore I shall have accomplished this, and consigned to them this fruit, I will come by you into Spain.
text.KJV: 15:28 When therefore I haue performed this, and hane sealed to them this fruit, I will come by you into Spaine.
text.NABRE: 15:28 So when I have completed this and safely handed over this contribution to them, I shall set out by way of you to Spain;

text.NRSV: and I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.
text.Rotherham: And know that, when I come unto you, in the fullness of the blessing of Christ, I shall come.
text.DRB: And I know, that when I come to you, I shall come in the abundance of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.
text.KJV: 15:29 And I am sure that when I come vnto you, I shall come in the fulnes of the blessing of þ Gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: 15:29 and I know that in coming to you I shall come in the fullness of Christ’s blessing.

text.NRSV: I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in earnest prayer to God on my behalf,
text.Rotherham: I beseech you, moreover, [brethren], through our Lord Jesus Christ, and through the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in your prayers on my behalf unto God,—
text.DRB: I beseech you therefore, brethren, through our Lord Jesus Christ, and by the charity of the Holy Ghost, that you help me in your prayers for me to God,
text.KJV: 15:30 Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Iesus Christs sake, and for the loue of the Spirit, that ye striue together with me, in your praiers to God for me,
text.NABRE: 15:30 I urge you, [brothers,] by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in the struggle by your prayers to God on my behalf,

text.NRSV: that I may be rescued from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my ministry to Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
text.Rotherham: In order that I may be delivered from them who do not yield in Judaea, and, my ministry which is unto Jerusalem, may prove to be acceptable unto the saints;
text.DRB: That I may be delivered from the unbelievers that are in Judea, and that the oblation of my service may be acceptable in Jerusalem to the saints.
text.KJV: 15:31 That I may bee deliuered from them that do not beleeue in Iudea, and that my seruice which I haue for Hierusalem, may bee accepted of the Saints:
text.NABRE: 15:31 that I may be delivered from the disobedient in Judea, and that my ministry for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the holy ones,

text.NRSV: so that by God’s will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed in your company.
text.Rotherham: In order that, with joy coming unto you through God’s will, I may together with you find rest.
text.DRB: That I may come to you with joy, by the will of God, and may be refreshed with you.
text.KJV: 15:32 That I may come vnto you with ioy by the will of God, and may with you be refreshed.
text.NABRE: 15:32 so that I may come to you with joy by the will of God and be refreshed together with you.

text.NRSV: The God of peace be with all of you. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Now, the God of peace, be with you all. Amen.
text.DRB: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
text.KJV: 15:33 Now the God of peace bee with you all. Amen.
text.NABRE: 15:33 The God of peace be with all of you. Amen.

text.KJV: 16:1 I commend vnto you Phebe our sister, which is a seruant of the Church which is at Cenchrea:
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 Phoebe Commended. I commend to you Phoebe our sister, who is [also] a minister of the church at Cenchreae,
text.NRSV: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a deacon of the church at Cenchreae,

text.KJV: 16:2 That ye receiue her in the Lord as becommeth Saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoeuer businesse she hath need of you: for she hath beene a succourer of many, and of my selfe also.
text.NABRE: 16:2 that you may receive her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the holy ones, and help her in whatever she may need from you, for she has been a benefactor to many and to me as well.
text.NRSV: so that you may welcome her in the Lord as is fitting for the saints, and help her in whatever she may require from you, for she has been a benefactor of many and of myself as well.

text.KJV: 16:3 Greete Priscilla and Aquila, my helpers in Christ Iesus:
text.NABRE: 16:3 Paul’s Greetings. Greet Prisca and Aquila, my co-workers in Christ Jesus,
text.NRSV: Greet Prisca and Aquila, who work with me in Christ Jesus,

text.KJV: 16:4 (Who haue for my life laid downe their owne neckes: vnto whome not onely I giue thankes, but also all the Churches of the Gentiles.)
text.NABRE: 16:4 who risked their necks for my life, to whom not only I am grateful but also all the churches of the Gentiles;
text.NRSV: and who risked their necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.

text.KJV: 16:5 Likewise greet the Church that is in their house. Salute my welbeloued Epenetus, who is the first fruits of Achaia vnto Christ.
text.NABRE: 16:5 greet also the church at their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the firstfruits in Asia for Christ.
text.NRSV: Greet also the church in their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.

text.KJV: 16:6 Greete Marie, who bestowed much labour on vs.
text.NABRE: 16:6 Greet Mary, who has worked hard for you.
text.NRSV: Greet Mary, who has worked very hard among you.

text.KJV: 16:7 Salute Andronicus and Iunia my kinsmen, and my fellow prisoners, who are of note among the Apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
text.NABRE: 16:7 Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners; they are prominent among the apostles and they were in Christ before me.
text.NRSV: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives who were in prison with me; they are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before I was.

text.KJV: 16:8 Greet Amplias my beloued in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:8 Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
text.NRSV: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.

text.KJV: 16:9 Salute Urbane our helper in Christ, and Stachys my beloued.
text.NABRE: 16:9 Greet Urbanus, our co-worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.
text.NRSV: Greet Urbanus, our co-worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.

text.KJV: 16:10 Salute Appelles approoued in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus houshold.
text.NABRE: 16:10 Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus.
text.NRSV: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus.

text.KJV: 16:11 Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the houshold of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:11 Greet my relative Herodion. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the family of Narcissus.
text.NRSV: Greet my relative Herodion. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the family of Narcissus.

text.KJV: 16:12 Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloued Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:12 Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet the beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
text.NRSV: Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet the beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.

text.KJV: 16:13 Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
text.NABRE: 16:13 Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
text.NRSV: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord; and greet his mother—a mother to me also.

text.KJV: 16:14 Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.
text.NABRE: 16:14 Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers who are with them.
text.NRSV: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.

text.KJV: 16:15 Salute Philologus & Iulia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the Saints which are with them.
text.NABRE: 16:15 Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the holy ones who are with them.
text.NRSV: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.

text.KJV: 16:16 Salute one another with an holy kisse. The Churches of Christ salute you.
text.NABRE: 16:16 Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
text.NRSV: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.

text.KJV: 16:17 Now I beseech you, brethren, marke them which cause diuisions and offences, contrary to the doctrine which ye haue learned, and auoide them.
text.NABRE: 16:17 Against Factions. I urge you, brothers, to watch out for those who create dissensions and obstacles, in opposition to the teaching that you learned; avoid them.
text.NRSV: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to keep an eye on those who cause dissensions and offenses, in opposition to the teaching that you have learned; avoid them.

text.KJV: 16:18 For they that are such, serue not our Lord Iesus Christ, but their owne belly, and by good wordes and faire speeches deceiue the hearts of the simple.
text.NABRE: 16:18 For such people do not serve our Lord Christ but their own appetites, and by fair and flattering speech they deceive the hearts of the innocent.
text.NRSV: For such persons do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own appetites, and by smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the simple-minded.

text.KJV: 16:19 For your obedience is come abroad vnto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalfe: but yet I would haue you wise vnto that which is good, and simple concerning euill.
text.NABRE: 16:19 For while your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, I want you to be wise as to what is good, and simple as to what is evil;
text.NRSV: For while your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, I want you to be wise in what is good and guileless in what is evil.

text.KJV: 16:20 And the God of peace shal bruise Satan vnder your feete shortly. The grace of our Lord Iesus Christ be with you. Amen.
text.NABRE: 16:20 then the God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
text.NRSV: The God of peace will shortly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

text.KJV: 16:21 Timotheus my worke-fellow, and Lucius, and Iason, and Sosipater my kinsemen salute you.
text.NABRE: 16:21 Greetings from Corinth. Timothy, my co-worker, greets you; so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my relatives.
text.NRSV: Timothy, my co-worker, greets you; so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my relatives.

text.KJV: 16:22 I Tertius who wrote this Epistle, salute you in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:22 I, Tertius, the writer of this letter, greet you in the Lord.
text.NRSV: I Tertius, the writer of this letter, greet you in the Lord.

text.KJV: 16:23 Gaius mine hoste, and of the whole Church, saluteth you. Erastus the Chamberlaine of the citie saluteth you, and Quartus a brother.
text.NABRE: 16:23 Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus greet you.
text.NRSV: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus, greet you.

text.KJV: 16:25 Now to him that is of power to stablish you according to my Gospel, and the preaching of Iesus Christ, according to the reuelation of the the mysterie, which was kept secret since the world began:
text.NABRE: 16:25 [ Now to him who can strengthen you, according to my gospel and the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery kept secret for long ages
text.NRSV: Now to God who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept secret for long ages

text.KJV: 16:26 But now is made manifest, and by the Scriptures of the Prophets according to the commandement of the euerlasting God, made knowen to all nations for the obedience of faith,
text.NABRE: 16:26 but now manifested through the prophetic writings and, according to the command of the eternal God, made known to all nations to bring about the obedience of faith,
text.NRSV: but is now disclosed, and through the prophetic writings is made known to all the Gentiles, according to the command of the eternal God, to bring about the obedience of faith—

text.KJV: 16:27 To God, onely wise, bee glorie through Iesus Christ, for euer. Amen.Written to the Romanes from Corinthus, and sent by Phebe seruant of the Church at Cenchrea.
text.NABRE: 16:27 to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever. Amen.]
text.NRSV: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever! Amen.

16.1: And I commend to you Phoebe our sister,—being a minister [also] of the assembly which is in Cenchreae;
16.2: In order that ye may give her welcome in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints, and stand by her in any matter wherein she may have need of you; for, she also, hath proved to be a defender of many, and of my own self.
16.3: Salute ye Prisca and Aquila, my fellow-workers in Christ Jesus,—
16.4: Who, indeed, for my life, their own neck laid down,—unto whom—not, I only, give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the nations;
16.5: [Salute] also the assembly at their house. Salute ye Epaenetus, my beloved, who is a first-fruit of Asia unto Christ.
16.6: Salute Mary—who, indeed, hath toiled much for you.
16.7: Salute Andronicus and Junias, my kinsmen and my fellow-captives, who, indeed, are of note among the Apostles, who also before me had come to be in Christ.
16.8: Salute Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
16.9: Salute Urbanus, our fellow-worker in Christ; and Stachys, my beloved.
16.10: Salute Apelles, the approved in Christ. Salute them of [the household of] Aristobulus.
16.11: Salute Herodion, my kinsman. Salute them of [the household of] Narcissus who are in the Lord.
16.12: Salute Tryphaena and Tryphosa, [sisters] who have toiled in the Lord. Salute Persis, the beloved [sister], who, indeed, hath toiled much in the Lord.
16.13: Salute Rufus, the chosen in the Lord; also his and my mother.
16.14: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brethren that are with them.
16.15: Salute Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympus, and all the saints that are with them.
16.16: Salute ye one another, with a holy a kiss. All the assemblies of the Christ salute you.
16.17: But I beseech you, brethren, to keep an eye upon them who are causing divisions and occasions of stumbling, aside from the teaching which, ye, have learned,—and be turning away from them;
16.18: For, they who are such, unto our Lord Christ are not doing service, but unto their own belly,—and, through their smooth and flattering speech, deceive the hearts of the innocent.
16.19: For, your obedience, unto all hath reached,—over you, therefore, I rejoice! But I wish you—to be wise, [indeed], as to that which is good, yet pure, as to that which is evil.
16.20: Howbeit, the God of peace, will crush Satan under your feet shortly. The favour of our Lord Jesus, be with you.
16.21: There salute you—Timothy, my fellow-worker, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
16.22: I, Tertius, who have written the epistle, salute you in the Lord.
16.23: There saluteth you—Gaius, my host and [the host] of the whole assembly. There salute you—Erastus, the steward of the city, and Quartus the brother.
16.25: Now, unto him who hath power to establish you, according to my glad-message—even the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of a sacred secret, in age-past times kept silent,
16.26: But now made manifest, and through means of prophetic scriptures, according to the command of the age-abiding God, for obedience of faith unto all the nations made known,
16.27: Unto a God, wise alone, through Jesus Christ, [unto whom] be the glory, unto the ages. Amen.

16.1: And I commend to you Phebe, our sister, who is in the ministry of the church, that is in Cenchrae:
16.2: That you receive her in the Lord as becometh saints; and that you assist her in whatsoever business she shall have need of you. For she also hath assisted many, and myself also.
16.3: Salute Prisca and Aquila, my helpers in Christ Jesus,
16.4: (Who have for my life laid down their own necks: to whom not I only give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles,)
16.5: And the church which is in their house. Salute Epenetus, my beloved: who is the firstfruits of Asia in Christ.
16.6: Salute Mary, who hath laboured much among you.
16.7: Salute Andronicus and Junias, my kinsmen and fellow prisoners: who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
16.8: Salute Ampliatus, most beloved to me in the Lord.
16.9: Salute Urbanus, our helper in Christ Jesus, and Stachys, my beloved.
16.11: Salute them that are of Aristobulus' household. Salute Herodian, my kinsman. Salute them that are of Narcissus' household, who are in the Lord.
16.12: Salute Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute Persis, the dearly beloved, who hath much laboured in the Lord.
16.13: Salute Rufus, elect in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
16.14: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren that are with them.
16.15: Salute Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympias; and all the saints that are with them.
16.16: Salute one another with an holy kiss. All the churches of Christ salute you.
16.17: Now I beseech you, brethren, to mark them who make dissensions and offences contrary to the doctrine which you have learned, and avoid them.
16.18: For they that are such, serve not Christ our Lord, but their own belly; and by pleasing speeches and good words, seduce the hearts of the innocent.
16.19: For your obedience is published in every place. I rejoice therefore in you. But I would have you to be wise in good, and simple in evil.
16.20: And the God of peace crush Satan under your feet speedily. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
16.21: Timothy, my fellow labourer, saluteth you, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
16.22: I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.
16.23: Caius, my host, and the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus, the treasurer of the city, saluteth you, and Quartus, a brother.
16.24: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
16.25: Now to him that is able to establish you, according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery, which was kept secret from eternity,
16.26: (Which now is made manifest by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the precept of the eternal God, for the obedience of faith,) known among all nations;
16.27: To God the only wise, through Jesus Christ, to whom be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.
